concept:
Tested 2 ? Which drug decreases LDL the most? HMG-CoA Reductase inhibitors (statins)
Which drug decreases triglycerides the most? Lipoprotein lipase stimulators (gemfibrozil, clofibrate)
What can cause sinus bradycardia?
CV conditioning (normal response)
Sinus node dysfunction
What are the ECG findings for bradycardia?
Rate < 60 bpm
Normal P waves before every QRS
What is the treatment for bradycardia?
Atropine
Pacemaker placement (definitive treatment)
Is right bundle branch block pathologic? No Yes
What can cause right bundle branch block
Can occur in normal individuals
COPD
Valvular disease
Chronic CAD
Status post surgical repair of VSD
What are the ECG findings of right bundle branch block?
QRS duration of > 0.12 seconds
RSR complex with wide R wave in V1
QRS pattern with a wide S wave in V6
What is the treatment for right bundle branch block? None needed
Is left bundle branch block pathologic? Yes No
What can cause left bundle branch block?
Organic heart disease
HTN
Valvular disease
Cardiomyopathy
CAD
What are the ECG findings for left bundle branch block?
QRS duration > 0.12 seconds
Wide, entirely negative QS complex in V1
Wide, tall R wave with no Q wave in V6
What is the cause of first degree heart block?
Can occur in normal individuals
Increased vagal tone
Lyme disease
What are the ECG findings in first degree heart block? PR interval > 0.2 seconds
What is the treatment for first degree heart block? None needed
What causes second degree heart block (Mobitz I)?
Drug effect (digoxin, Beta-blockaged, Ca-channel blockers)
Increased vagal tone
What are the ECG findings for second degree heart block (Mobitz I)?
Increasing PR interval until a dropped beat occurs.
PR then resets
What is the treatment for second degree heart block (Mobitz I)? Stop the offending drug
What is the cause of second degree heart block (Mobitz II )?
Fibrotic disease of the conduction system
Previous septal MI
Lyme Disease
What are the signs and symptoms of second degree heart block (Mobitz II)?
Occasionally syncope
Progression to third degree AV block
What are the ECG findings of second degree heart block (Mobitz II)?
Unexpected dropped beat
No changes in the PR interval
What is the treatment for second degree heart block (Mobitz II)? Pacemaker placement
What causes third degree (complete) heart block? No electrical communications between the atria and ventricles
What are the ECG findings for third degree (complete) heart block?
No relationship between P waves and QRS complexes
Wide QRS complexes
What is the treatment for third degree (complete) heart block)? Pacemaker placement
Define malignant hypertension: Is one or more of:
Progressive renal failure
Encephalopathy with papilledema
What valvular lesion is associated with:
Exertional dyspnea
Angina
syncope?
Aortic Stenosis
What are the risk factors for aortic stenosis?
Rheumatic heart disease
Congenital aortic stenosis
Bicuspid aortic valve
What valvular lesion is associated with midsystolic crescendo-decrescendo murmur heard best at second intercostal space, radiating to the neck? Aortic stenosis
What PE signs are associated with aortic stenosis?
Pulsus parvus et tardus (weak, delayed carotid upstroke)
Sustained apical beat
Paradoxically split S2
What is the treatment for aortic stenosis?
Avoid afterload reducers.
Valve replacement is curative
What valvular lesion is associated with LVH, angina, LHF due to volume overload? Aortic regurge
What are the risk factors for aortic regurge?
Rheumatic heart disease
VSD
Infective endocarditis
Congenital bicuspid aortic valve
Marfan's
What valvular lesion is associated with a high-pitched blowing diastolic murmur at left sternal border? Aortic regurge
What valvular lesion is associated with a low-pitched mid-diastolic rumble? Aortic regurge
